name: Ruta de la Muela por el Monasterio del Olivar — circular desde Estercuel (BTT05)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Estercuel offers diverse terrain at the piedmont of the Sierra de San Just, characterized by river valleys, canyons, and gorges. The landscape features unique arboreal formations like the "Barrancondo," with Aleppo pines and holm oaks, alongside geologically interesting former mining "desmontes." Riverside forests with poplars and willows provide shaded routes, contributing to a varied experience for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Estercuel The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Majalinos Route, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain at the foot of the Sierra de San Just. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Masías de Ejulve BTT 01 Trail, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route winds through diverse vegetation and offers views of the surrounding valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail, a 16.9 miles (27.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and canyons, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Estercuel is defined by its piedmont terrain, river valleys with canyons, and unique arboreal formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Estercuel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Estercuel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Estercuel?
A: Estercuel offers a network of over 14 mountain bike trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. The routes traverse diverse terrain, from river valleys to the piedmont of the Sierra de San Just.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for different skill levels in Estercuel?
A: Yes, the trails around Estercuel accommodate various skill levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Estercuel?
A: Mountain biking in Estercuel will take you through diverse landscapes including river valleys, canyons, and gorges at the foot of the Sierra de San Just. You can also experience unique arboreal formations like the 'Barrancondo' with its Aleppo pines and holm oaks, and the geologically interesting former mining 'desmontes' which create a unique, almost 'science fiction' like landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, you can cycle to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;, located just 4 km from Estercuel. This 16th-17th century monastery is nestled in a river valley surrounded by pine forests, offering a cultural highlight amidst natural beauty. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996657655/ruta-de-la-muela-por-el-monasterio-del-olivar-circular-desde-estercuel-btt05" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Muela por el Monasterio del Olivar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular trail that passes by it.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Estercuel?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain foothills, and the unique natural formations that make each ride distinctive.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999030439/sierra-de-majalinos-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Majalinos Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.1-mile (61.3 km) trail that typically takes over 6 hours to complete, traversing varied terrain at the foot of the Sierra de San Just.

Q: Can I find a moderate mountain bike route that offers diverse scenery?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996732292/masias-de-ejulve-btt-01-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masías de Ejulve BTT 01 Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 28.1-mile (45.2 km) path that winds through diverse vegetation and offers scenic views of the surrounding valleys.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate mountain bike trails in Estercuel?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999033798/rio-escuriza-el-campillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.9-mile (27.1 km) route that leads through river valleys and canyons, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Estercuel?
A: Given its location in Aragon, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Estercuel, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, while winters may bring colder temperatures, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Estercuel?
A: Yes, Estercuel is a small village and typically offers parking options for visitors, often near the town center or trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas.

Q: Do I need a permit to mountain bike in Estercuel?
A: Generally, you do not need a specific permit for mountain biking on established trails in the Estercuel area of Aragon, Spain. However, always respect private property, follow local regulations, and stay on marked paths to protect the natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Estercuel?
A: Yes, you can find circular routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996657655/ruta-de-la-muela-por-el-monasterio-del-olivar-circular-desde-estercuel-btt05" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Muela por el Monasterio del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 24.7-kilometer (15.3-mile) loop starting and ending in Estercuel.
tips:
The Sanctuary/Monastery of Santa María del Olivar is located in Estercuel, within the natural setting of the Serranía de Montalbán mountain range, in the Andorra-Sierra de Arcos region.

Although often referred to as a sanctuary, its official name is the Monastery of Santa María del Olivar. It is one of the most important historical and artistic monuments in the area and has been a significant spiritual center for over seven centuries.
Located 4 km from Estercuel, in the province of Teruel, it lies within the river valley and is surrounded by pine forests.
It sits atop a hill at an altitude of 700 meters, overlooking the valley watered by the Escuriza River.

The pretty town of Estercuel is located in the Andorra-Sierra de Arcos region, in the province of Teruel. The Olivar Monastery, not far from the town, is the biggest tourist attraction. We can also visit the coal mine, the Ermita del Olivar or the fire and fiesta interpretation center.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar
